Package component.modifier.changer

Class ValueSetter

java.lang.Object
component.modifier.Modifier
component.modifier.changer.Changer<T>
component.modifier.changer.Setter<Integer>
component.modifier.changer.ValueSetter
All Implemented Interfaces:
Tippable, GridIndexable
public class ValueSetter extends Setter<Integer>
The ValueSetter class is a specialized Setter that overwrites a Card's numerical value.

Unlike Arithmetic modifiers, which transform values relatively, this setter forces a card to a specific rank regardless of its previous value.

  • Constructor Details

    • ValueSetter

      public ValueSetter(int changeValue)
      Constructs a ValueSetter with a target rank.
      Parameters:
      changeValue - The numerical value [1-13] to be assigned to cards.

  • Method Details

    • change

      public void change(Card toModify)
      Forces the target card's value to the rank stored in this modifier.
      Specified by:
      change in class Changer<Integer>
      Parameters:
      toModify - The Card whose value will be overwritten.

    • setChange

      public void setChange(Integer change)
      Updates the target value for this setter, enforcing standard card rank bounds.

      Uses Math.clamp to ensure the target value remains between 1 (Ace) and 13 (King).

      Overrides:
      setChange in class Changer<Integer>
      Parameters:
      change - The desired target integer.

    • getTooltip

      public Tooltip getTooltip()
      Generates a descriptive tooltip for the Value Setter.

      Includes a dynamic reference to the target rank and indicates if the modifier has been disabled by external logic (like a Corrupted card).

      Specified by:
      getTooltip in interface Tippable
      Overrides:
      getTooltip in class Changer<Integer>
      Returns:
      A Tooltip detailing the setter's behavior.
